The following is a list of test cases and their corresponding test steps for a system that identifies trace links between software requirements and system tests:

1. Test case T-0: To verify that the AG can establish a complete audio connection towards the HS.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. HS is in Standby mode, no connection to AG. AG is in Standby mode, no active call and no connection to HS. AG is configured to pass an incoming call/audio connection to the HS.
   Procedure: AG: Initiate a call/audio connection to the AG from a test device. HS: The HS or AG might be alerted. In that case establish the connection by the corresponding user action (e.g. press button) at the HS. Expected Outcome AG: A call/audio connection is established.

2. Test case T-1: To verify that the HS accepts a complete connection establishment initiated from the AG.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. HS is in Standby mode, no connection to AG. AG is in Standby mode, no active call and no connection to HS. AG is configured to pass an incoming call/audio connection to the HS.
   Procedure: AG: Initiate a call/audio connection to the AG from a test device. HS: The HS or AG might be alerted. In that case establish the connection by the corresponding user action (e.g. press button) at the HS. Expected Outcome HS: A call/audio connection is established.

3. Test case T-2: To verify that, if supported and provided by the AG, the AG can cause the HS to give an in-band ringing tone during connection establishment initiated from the AG.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. HS is in Standby mode, no connection to AG. AG is in Standby mode, no active call and no connection to HS. AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: AG: Initiate a call/audio connection to the AG from a test device. HS: Upon alerting in the HS earpiece, establish the connection by the corresponding user action (e.g. press button) at the HS. Expected Outcome AG: A call/audio connection is established.

4. Test case T-3: To verify that, if supported and provided by the AG, the HS gives an in-band ringing tone during connection establishment initiated from the AG.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. HS is in Standby mode, no connection to AG. AG is in Standby mode, no active call and no connection to HS. AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: AG: Initiate a call/audio connection to the AG from a test device. HS: Upon alerting in the HS earpiece, establish the connection by the corresponding user action (e.g. press button) at the HS. Expected Outcome HS: The user action on the HS establishes the connection and bi-directional conversation is possible.

5. Test case T-4: To verify that the AG accepts a complete connection establishment initiated from the HS.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. HS is in Standby mode, no connection to AG. AG is in Standby mode, no active call and no connection to HS. AG is prepared for establishing outgoing connections upon user action on the HS (e.g. last dialed or pre-programmed number). The manufacturer must state, which options for determination of the number are implemented. It is sufficient to test with one of these.
   Procedure: HS: Initiate the user action (e.g. press button) on the HS to establish an outgoing call/audio connection. AG: No user action is required. Expected Outcome AG: A call/audio connection is established.

6. Test case T-5: To verify that the HS can establish a complete audio connection towards the AG.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. HS is in Standby mode, no connection to AG. AG is in Standby mode, no active call and no connection to HS. AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: Initiate the user action (e.g. press button) on the HS to establish an outgoing call/audio connection. AG: No user action is required. Expected Outcome HS: A call/audio connection is established.

7. Test case T-6: To verify that the AG accepts a connection release initiated from the headset.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. For HS a call/audio connection is active with audio I/O on HS. For AG a call/audio connection is active (voice I/O on HS). AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: Initiate the user action (e.g. press button) on the HS to release the connection. AG: No user action is required. Expected Outcome AG: A call/audio connection is established.

8. Test case T-7: To verify that the AG accepts a connection release initiated from the headset.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. For HS a call/audio connection is active with audio I/O on HS. For AG a call/audio connection is active (voice I/O on HS). AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: Initiate the user action (e.g. press button) on the HS to release the connection. AG: Initiate the user action or internal event to release the connection. Expected Outcome HS: The user action on the HS releases the connection.

9. Test case T-8: To verify that the HS accepts an audio connection release initiated on the AG.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. For HS a call/audio connection is active with audio I/O on HS. For AG a call/audio connection is active (voice I/O on HS). AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: No user action is required. AG: Initiate the user action or internal event to release the connection. Expected Outcome HS: The user action on the AG releases the audio connection.

10. Test case T-9: To verify that the AG can perform an audio connection transfer from AG to HS initiated by a user action on the headset.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. For HS a call/audio connection is active with audio I/O on HS. For AG a call/audio connection is active (voice I/O on HS). AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: Initiate the user action (e.g. press button) on the HS to transfer the audio connection from AG to HS. AG: No user action is required. Expected Outcome AG: The user action on the HS transfers the audio connection from AG to HS.

11. Test case T-10: To verify that the HS accepts an audio connection transfer from HS to AG initiated by a user action on the AG.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. For HS a call/audio connection is active with audio I/O on HS. For AG a call/audio connection is active (voice I/O on HS). AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: No user action is required. AG: Initiate the user action or internal event to transfer the audio connection from HS to AG. Expected Outcome HS: The user action on the AG transfers the audio connection from HS to AG. The HS returns to Standby mode.

12. Test case T-12: To verify that the AG can perform an audio connection transfer from AG to HS initiated by a user action on the headset.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. For HS a call/audio connection is active with audio I/O on HS. For AG a call/audio connection is active (voice I/O on HS). AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: Initiate the user action (e.g. press button) on the HS to transfer the audio connection from AG to HS. AG: No user action is required. Expected Outcome AG: The user action on the HS transfers the audio connection from AG to HS. The HS returns to Standby mode.

13. Test case T-13: To verify that the AG accepts an audio connection transfer from HS to AG initiated by a user action on the AG.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. For HS a call/audio connection is active with audio I/O on HS. For AG a call/audio connection is active (voice I/O on HS). AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: No user action is required. AG: Initiate the user action or internal event to transfer the audio connection from HS to AG. Expected Outcome HS: The user action on the AG transfers the audio connection from HS to AG. The AG returns to Standby mode.

14. Test case T-14: To verify that the AG can perform an audio connection transfer from AG to HS initiated by a user action on the headset.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. For HS a call/audio connection is active with audio I/O on HS. For AG a call/audio connection is active (voice I/O on HS). AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: Initiate the user action (e.g. press button) on the HS to transfer the audio connection from AG to HS. AG: No user action is required. Expected Outcome AG: The user action on the HS transfers the audio connection from AG to HS. The HS returns to Standby mode.

15. Test case T-15: To verify that the HS accepts an audio connection transfer from AG to HS initiated by a user action on the AG.
   Initial condition: Both devices are initilized. Ensure HS and AG are initially paired, confirming interoperability for the headset profile. For HS a call/audio connection is active with audio I/O on HS. For AG a call/audio connection is active (voice I/O on HS). AG is configured to provide an in-band ring tone.
   Procedure: HS: Initiate the user action (e.g. press button) on the HS to transfer the audio connection from AG to HS. AG: No user action is required. Expected Outcome HS: The user action on the AG transfers the audio connection from AG to HS. The HS returns to Standby mode.

The following is a list of test IDs that cover the requirement for remote audio volume control: T-0, T-1, T-2, T-3, T-4, T-5, T-6, T-7, T-8.

The requirement states that the AG can control the gain of the microphone or speaker of the HS by sending unsolicited result codes +VGM or +VGS respectively. The requirement also states that support for remote audio volume control is optional, and both the peaker and microphone gain are represented as parameters to the +VGM and +VGS, on a scale from 0 to 15. The values are absolute values, relating to a particular (implementation-dependent) volume level controlled by the HS. The HS may store the VGS and VGM settings at connection release. At connection establishment, the HS may restore the previous settings using the AT+VGS and AT+VGM commands. In case physical mechanisms (buttons, dials etc.) means are implemented on the HS to control the volume levels, the HS should also use the AT+VGS and AT+VGM commands to inform the AG of any changes in the volume levels.

The following test IDs cover the requirement for remote audio volume control: T-0, T-1, T-2, T-3, T-4, T-5, T-6, T-7.

The requirement states that the AG can cause the HS to give an in-band ringing tone during connection establishment initiated from the AG. The requirement also states that the AG has to be prepared for establishing outgoing connections upon user action on the HS. The manufacturer must state which options for determination of the number are implemented. It is sufficient to test with one of these options.

The following test IDs cover the requirement for the AG causing the HS to give an in-band ringing tone during connection establishment initiated from the AG: T-2, T-3.

The requirement states that the HS can establish a complete audio connection towards the AG if the AG initiates a call/audio connection to the HS from a test device. The requirement also states that the HS may store the VGS and VGM settings at connection release and restore them using the AT+VGS and AT+VGM commands. In case physical mechanisms (buttons, dials etc.) are implemented on the HS to control the volume levels, the HS should also use the AT+VGS and AT+VGM commands to inform the AG of any changes in the volume levels.

The following test IDs cover the requirement for the HS establishing a complete audio connection towards the AG: T-4, T-5, T-6, T-7.

The requirement states that the HS can accept a connection release initiated from the headset. The requirement also states that the HS may send an AT+VGS or AT+VGM command to the AG to inform it of any changes in the volume levels. The requirement also states that the HS may provide no other alert besides the playback of in-band ring. User action on the HS establishes the connection and bi-directional conversation or any other intended audio application is possible.

The following test IDs cover the requirement for the HS accepting a connection release initiated from the headset: T-6, T-8.

The requirement states that the AG can accept a connection release initiated from the HS. The requirement also states that the AG may send an AT+VGS or AT+VGM command to the HS to inform it of any changes in the volume levels. The requirement also states that the AG may provide no other alert besides the playback of in-band ring. User action on the HS establishes the connection and bi-directional conversation or any other intended audio application is possible.

The following test IDs cover the requirement for the AG accepting a connection release initiated from the HS: T-9, T-10.
